hypothesis: If AMDEN—an EGNN-backboned diffusion model trained on MD-generated amorphous structures—uses energy-based noise prediction with HMC refinement during reverse denoising, then it will produce amorphous materials whose radial distribution functions, bond-angle distributions, structure factors, and potential energy distributions match MD reference ensembles more closely (before and after local geometry optimization) than those produced by standard denoising, because energy-based scores plus HMC equilibration enforce more physically plausible local geometries.
